summary: DHH announces Backpack, 37signals' next product after Basecamp, describing it as a personal organization tool. He frames it as 'Basecamp for your life' — a system for getting your life organized that goes beyond strict productivity frameworks like Getting Things Done. He acknowledges the difficulty of explaining the product in words and emphasizes that examples and demos are the best way to understand it. The post builds anticipation for the upcoming marketing site launch.
key_points: Backpack is positioned as 'Basecamp for your life' — personal organization rather than project management
The product defies easy categorization, being broader than pure productivity tools like GTD systems
37signals believes in showing over telling — examples and demos convey the product better than descriptions
The launch strategy uses scarcity and anticipation with 'golden tickets' for early access
money_quotes: Backpack is Basecamp for your life.
It's a Get Your Shit Together And Keep It So kind of system.
This is really one of those things were words are poor substitutes for experience, which is why the marketing site is all about examples.
All the people we've been demoing Backpack to got it as soon as they started seeing how we used it.
key_insight: The best products are often the hardest to describe in words — they're understood through experience, not explanation.
